purpose: Production caching layer for session data, rate limit counters, and response caching

configuration.evictionAction: REQUIRED: Enable eviction via Upstash Console (https://console.upstash.com/redis/f72c073a-f32f-4ae8-844d-ce5d7b82b2d7) > Settings > Eviction toggle. This sets maxmemory-policy to allkeys-lru.
configuration.currentMaxmemoryPolicy: noeviction (needs to be changed to allkeys-lru via console)

rateLimiting.description: Per-endpoint rate limit counters (100/min general, 20/min auth, 300/min scanner)

responseCache.description: Cached API responses (event details: 5min, CMS sections: 5min, guest list: 1min)

capacityPlanning.memoryEstimate: ~40MB for 50 concurrent events with average key sizes

connectionSettings.gracefulDegradationNote: API continues operating without cache on connection failure

environmentVariables.UPSTASH_REDIS_CACHE_REST_TOKEN: Stored in secret manager - do not hardcode

rdbSnapshots.note: Upstash manages RDB persistence at the platform level. On the free tier, Upstash automatically handles data persistence internally — users cannot configure custom RDB snapshot intervals (e.g., every 15 minutes). The platform ensures data survives restarts.
rdbSnapshots.resolution: Upstash's managed persistence provides equivalent durability. Custom RDB intervals are not user-configurable on any Upstash tier — this is handled transparently by the platform.

aofPersistence.note: Upstash handles AOF-equivalent persistence internally. Users cannot configure fsync policies directly.

dailyBackups.note: Daily backups require a paid Upstash plan. Attempted to enable via mcp_upstash_redis_database_set_daily_backup but received: 'only paid databases can use backup feature'
dailyBackups.actionRequired: Upgrade database to paid tier, then enable daily backups via Upstash Console or MCP tool: mcp_upstash_redis_database_set_daily_backup with database_id f72c073a-f32f-4ae8-844d-ce5d7b82b2d7

separation.note: For current scale (1 event, ≤500 guests), this single instance handles BOTH cache and pub/sub. Socket.io adapter connects to this same instance via UPSTASH_REDIS_CACHE_URL fallback. At this scale, pub/sub traffic is negligible and won't cause eviction pressure.
separation.scaleThreshold: Consider separating when: multiple concurrent events, 1000+ guests, or observed cache eviction due to pub/sub memory pressure
